name: Ame-no-Tajikarao
shortDescription: Ame-no-Tajikarao is a powerful kami in Japanese mythology, known for his immense strength and role in retrieving Amaterasu from the Ama-no-Iwato.
description: Ame-no-Tajikarao is a prominent deity in Japanese Shinto mythology, primarily known for his exceptional physical strength. His most famous role is in the myth of Amaterasu, the sun goddess, retreating into the Ama-no-Iwato (heavenly rock cave), plunging the world into darkness. When the other deities devised a plan to lure Amaterasu out, Ame-no-Tajikarao hid near the entrance. As Amaterasu peeked out, drawn by the festivities and Uzume's dance, Ame-no-Tajikarao seized the opportunity and forcefully pulled her from the cave, preventing her from retreating again. He is often depicted as a strong, muscular figure, symbolizing power and determination. As the one who physically moved the rock, he is associated with the opening of new possibilities and overcoming obstacles. His association with the Ama-no-Iwato myth connects him to themes of purification (harae) and the removal of defilement (kegare), as the world's darkness was lifted upon Amaterasu's return. He is also associated with Izumo Shrine, a place of great importance in Japanese mythology.
epithets: The God Who Opens the Rock Door
